findings: GAP: teardown updates only run-level state.json and does not mark commander swarm-state.json or child-agents.jsonl, so commander progress can remain stuck at running during stop/recovery (bin/stampede.sh:304-363).

GAP: teardown kills only tracked commander/worker pane PIDs; nested child agents spawned under commanders are not tracked by the launcher PID registry and may survive teardown (bin/stampede.sh:327-339,1276-1304).

GAP: launcher errors only when TASK_COUNT is zero and does not verify TASK_COUNT >= WORKER_COUNT/COMMANDER_COUNT, so metaswarm can over-launch panes relative to queued work without forcing partial/failed state (bin/stampede.sh:700-731).
NOTE: no additional bounded-spawn failure gap was observed in agents/stampede-commander.agent.md beyond the recursion and enforcement issues already flagged by other workers.
